en: Does the company have a policy statement, program or management system that confirms a commitment to preventing modern slavery, forced labor and human trafficking within the company’s operations and supply chain?
Does this policy statement cover anti-slavery practices?
Does the management commitment  confirm, that the following classification of modern slavery and forced labor are prohibited within the business and its supply chain?
Does this statement and all of the prohibited practices relating to forced labor extend to subcontractors, agents and other third-parties involved with the delivery of company services?

To assist the review personnel assessing your company documentation, please ensure the following criteria is met:
• Company name, or parent company, as recorded within your Avetta account.
• All information and documents must be submitted in English.
• All documents must confirm the date they were produced, or subject to review before approval by the company (documents should preferably be no older than 2 years old).
• Where applicable include a signature of senior member of the management team e.g. CEO / President / Managing Director (Confirming the company intent and motivation to deliver a specific requirement).
• Uploaded documents and files should be in a searchable text format (PDF, Word, Txt). Policy and program information found on your company’s website, or via an hyperlink, should be converted and submit as electronic documents. Scanned documents or image captures will be rejected.
Information provided to support your responses will be assessed based upon the documentary title, even if it may be part of a larger program or standardized manual. Anything different from a standard approach may be rejected and not scored.
Please upload a policy statement / document related to the prevention of modern slavery, forced labor and human trafficking:
Please explain why there is no policy / statement currently in place.
Company believes this policy is not required based on current scope of work
Company does not believe it is big enough to justify this category/type of policy
Current company management programs currently under review
Company currently developing modern slavery statement/policy and it will be implemented within the next 12 months
Company currently developing modern slavery statement/policy and it will be implemented within the next 6 months
Can the company confirm that it complies with national and international legislation relating to modern slavery, forced labor, and human trafficking?

Has the company appointed a senior management representative responsible for the implementation, enforcement and compliance of the company policy statement / management program regarding the prevention of modern slavery and forced labor?\n
Has the company or any company within the supply chain been found to have identified issues around modern slavery or forced labor?
Issues relating to modern slavery and forced labor were identified:
Found within the supply chain – direct supplier
Found within the supply chain – indirect supplier
Found within supply chain – non-contracted supplier
Found within primary country of operation

Please confirm how the supplier code of conduct is applied.
Applies to all levels of the company's supply chain
Content confirms the principles of doing business with the company
Is publicly available (e.g. displayed on the company's website)
Suppliers do not receive any business if the suppliers do not recognize and accept the requirements of the code

Commitment to reduce energy / resource usage

A framework for setting objectives and targets to improve company environmental performance
Compliance with environmental laws  for all locations where the company operates

Provide independent consultancy services to assist suppliers

Does the company work with suppliers to resolve issues that may have been identified during their ethical sourcing evaluation?\n\nMethods used by the company to assist suppliers to resolve issues / events identified from ethical evaluations:
Provide 3rd party / independent support to rectify the issue / event
Work directly with supplier to source alternative providers of services / materials
Work directly with supplier to initiate action plan to mitigate potential business impact
Instruct company legal counsel to advise supplier

(e.g., sourcing raw materials / processing / manufacturing / production / assembly and transportation)

A living-wage provides workers with an income level that allows them to pay for adequate housing / shelter, food and essentials e.g., basic medical care. This should not be confused with a minimum wage that may be regulated / mandated by a government agency. The living wage normally exceeds the minimum level.
Does the company pay workers a “living-wage”  in the country of operation?
Can your company demonstrate workers are paid the national minimum wage in the country of operation?
Please explain why the company does not pay the minimum wage as part of the remuneration payment of workers:
All company workers are paid in excess of the minimum wage
All company workers are paid in excess of the minimum wage (living wage only)
No mandatory minimum wage required in country of operation
Wages calculated based upon production process and worker performance/individual output e.g., piecework
Additional information, details, answers, or documents entered under this question may be subject to verification / examination, but will not be considered in the overall ESG Assessment score

Can the company confirm there are management controls that prevents the recruitment and use of underage labor (Under the age of 15 years) within any part of the business?
Can the company demonstrate that the controls are supported by  management systems/procedures and a company statement confirming a "zero-tolerance" to recruitment of child labor?

This includes demonstrating a “zero-tolerance “ to the use of child labor within supplier organizations contracted to provide a service, and their suppliers who support them, this can be in the form of formal policy, mandatory contract requirement, etc.
Can the company confirm the controls and management commitment to not using child labor is applied to all parts of the supply chain?

This can include procedures, wording from contracts  and company policy statements advising on the company attitude to the use of underage labor and how it is applied to the selection and use of subcontractors and suppliers.

To assist the review personnel assessing your company documentation, please ensure the following criteria is met:
• Company name, or parent company, as recorded within your Avetta account.
• All information and documents must be submitted in English.
• All documents must confirm the date they were produced, or subject to review before approval by the company (documents should preferably be no older than 2 years old).
• Where applicable include a signature of senior member of the management team e.g. CEO / President / Managing Director (Confirming the company intent and motivation to deliver a specific requirement).
• Uploaded documents and files should be in a searchable text format (PDF, Word, Txt). Policy and program information found on your company’s website, or via an hyperlink, should be converted and submit as electronic documents. Scanned documents or image captures will be rejected.
Information provided to support your responses will be assessed based upon the documentary title, even if it may be part of a larger program or standardized manual. Anything different from a standard approach may be rejected and not scored.
